title: WIP: Experiment with Cirrus
body:
- Migrate the macOS task ~as is, without modifying (TO DO: or even renaming) the script)
- Smoke-test the out-of-container parts of local build

comments.body:
Re: 'macOS' failures...I didn't look at the test code, but from the output I'm speculating if this might be due to a missing brew package?  @ashley-cui and I worked on getting the native MacOS builds working in podman CI and I seem to remember there being problems relating to crypto/signing.

Here's what I'm trying for something similar in buildah ci:

```yaml
cross_build_task:
    name: "Cross Compile"
    alias: cross_build

    depends_on:
      - unit

    osx_instance:
        image: 'catalina-base'

    script:
        - brew install go
        - brew install go-md2man
        - brew install gpgme
        - make cross CGO_ENABLED=0

    binary_artifacts:
        path: ./bin/*
```

Hint: There's a way to have Cirrus-CI run a testing script inside a container image which it manages.  Cirrus will cache the image after the first build, and only rebuild it if the `Dockerfile` changes or if the image gets purged due to inactivity.

Docs: https://cirrus-ci.org/guide/docker-builder-vm/#dockerfile-as-a-ci-environment

@mtrmac I spent quite a bit of time looking at the output and test code...with little headway :disappointed: 

Do we get any more helpful output of the failure if you remove (even temporarily) the `| grep -v '^github\.com/containers/skopeo/\(integration\|vendor/.*\)$'` on the `test-unit-local` target?

@cevich If you are talking about the “key not found” failure on macOS, I haven’t looked at that yet at all. My first guess is something in the environment or permissions that causes `gpg` to ignore the files in `cmd/skopeo/fixtures`; testing that separately without involving the `gpgme` library might be easier than getting and understanding logs from that library.

ya, `gpgme` (and gpg) isn't always straight-forward to diagnose.  I'm not aware of anything in `gpg` that would cause it to ignore files by default.  Unless otherwise instructed, permissions there should be the same as what was used to clone the repo.   The reference to something called `/dev/full` makes me think it's something in the environment as well.  Perhaps the version of something changed WRT what was being done before in Travis?
@cevich could you take this PR over and finish getting skopeo under cirrus?
I'll take a look and try to figure out why the Mac stuff is failing :sweat_smile:
@ashley-cui any news? Were you able to reproduce the failure?
@cevich I'm so sorry I got way too wrapped up in secrets work, I definitely will take a look this afternoon and update here on my findings

@cevich Okay, took a little look at it. It's just that the test here: https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/84232cf3069a1b2f94792a28c9c980cea68ac356/cmd/skopeo/signing_test.go#L76
uses `/dev/full` to test an output error. Unfortunately, `/dev/full` which doesn't exist on a Mac. So if we either skip that test on a Mac or use something else to test a bad output, everything should work on the Mac.

@ashley-cui Hmmm, [what if we did like this](https://www.thedroidsonroids.com/blog/dev-full-osx), and made a symlink to `/dev/full`  like they suggest, before running the tests?

Not having a Mac at hand, it's hard for me to try any of this out.  Does the script in the article work as-written for you?
@cevich tested the script and it looks to work as-written :)

> @ashley-cui Hmmm, [what if we did like this](https://www.thedroidsonroids.com/blog/dev-full-osx), and made a symlink to `/dev/full` like they suggest, before running the tests?

That test only exercises an “`io.WriteFile` failed” error path, without caring all that much _how_ it fails. That path can be quite easily reached by providing an existing but non-writable (0400 perms) file, or a `/path/to/a/file/in/a/nonexistent/directory`. Especially the latter one should be very portable without needing any OS-specific infrastructure.

----

The `key "1D8230F6CDB6A06716E414C1DB72F2188BB46CC8" not found` failure might be more tricky; maybe https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1151#issuecomment-762321813 , see if/how `GNUPGHOME=… gpg --sign …` fails. (But I still haven’t tried looking into it myself; it works on my personal Mac fine, and it works fine in Travis, this is something to do with the Cirrus CI environment.)

> this is something to do with the Cirrus CI environment.

Suggestion: in travis and cirrus, print all the environment variables (`printenv`).  Then compare. Maybe it's something simple like Cirrus not setting `$HOME` or something.

@lsm5 I think the next step is easy: Clone and push your own copy of this PR (then close this one).  Then there are two suggestions above, [one for fixing the broken test, another hinting at some gpg/environment problem.](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1151#issuecomment-767107627)

> @lsm5 I think the next step is easy: Clone and push your own copy of this PR (then close this one). Then there are two suggestions above, [one for fixing the broken test, another hinting at some gpg/environment problem.](https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1151#issuecomment-767107627)

will do, thanks! :+1:
